question_title: What will be the maximum intraday value of the VIX over these biweekly periods in Q1 2026? (Jan 19 - Jan 30)
summary: Forecasting the maximum intraday VIX value during Jan 19-30, 2026. Starting VIX is 14.51 (Jan 2 close), well below the long-run mean of ~17. The 9-trading-day target period includes an FOMC meeting (likely Jan 27-28) and ongoing Q4 earnings season — both known volatility catalysts. I ran 200,000-path Monte Carlo simulations using three model variants (base Ornstein-Uhlenbeck, enhanced volatility for FOMC/earnings, and jump-diffusion for tail risk), blended 25/50/25. Mean reversion from 14.5 toward 17 is the dominant force pulling the distribution upward. The max of 9 daily intraday highs naturally creates a right-skewed distribution. The median max intraday high is approximately 17.5, with the central 60% interval spanning roughly 15.5-20.5. Upper tail risk is non-trivial due to FOMC reaction potential and possible geopolitical/market shocks. Final percentiles adjusted slightly upward from the blended MC to account for VIX fat tails that OU processes may underestimate.

factors.description: Mean reversion: VIX at 14.51 is significantly below long-run mean of ~17, creating strong upward pull over the 19-day horizon to target period
FOMC meeting Jan 27-28: Known volatility catalyst that historically causes 10-20% VIX moves on announcement day, falls within target period
Q4 earnings season in full swing: Major tech/financial earnings in late January add uncertainty and potential for VIX spikes
Max of 9 daily highs: Taking the maximum of multiple correlated observations naturally produces right-skewed distribution above mean daily levels
Recent downtrend and low-vol regime: VIX has fallen from ~26 to ~14.5 over 5 weeks; low-vol regimes can be sticky
VIX fat tails: Geopolitical shocks, market dislocations, or surprise economic data could push VIX to 25-35+ range (jump-diffusion model: 2-4% probability)

**Maximum intraday VIX, Jan 19-30, 2026**

| Percentile | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| 10th | 14.5 |
| 20th | 15.5 |
| 40th | 16.8 |
| **50th (median)** | **~17.5** |
| 60th | 18.2 |
| 80th | 20.5 |
| 90th | 23.0 |

**Key drivers:**
- **Mean reversion** is the dominant force — VIX at 14.5 is well below the ~17 long-run mean
- **FOMC meeting (Jan 27-28)** is the primary volatility catalyst in the target period
- **Earnings season** adds background uncertainty
- **Right-skewed distribution** reflects the asymmetric upside risk of VIX (can spike much more than it can fall)
- The 80% confidence interval spans [14.5, 23.0], with most mass in the 15-21 range
